name: Central Atlas Tamazight

ancestors.notes: Descent per Glottolog classification of Central Moroccan Berber (cent2194).

notes: Skeleton. Central Atlas Tamazight, a Berber (Afroasiatic) language of Morocco written in IRCAM Neo-Tifinagh. Letters are mapped per the IRCAM standard, including the pharyngealized series ⴹ ⵟ ⵚ ⵕ ⵥ, the pharyngeals ⵃ ⵄ, uvulars ⵅ ⵇ, and the labiovelar digraphs ⴳⵯ ⴽⵯ (the ⵯ modifier is mapped to the labialization diacritic). Unlike an abjad, vowels a/i/u and schwa ⴻ are written, though schwa is often omitted in practice; consonant gemination is not marked in the script and is left unencoded.

sources.author: Hammarström, H. et al. (Glottolog 5.0)
sources.title: Glottolog: Central Moroccan Berber (cent2194)
sources.publisher: Max Planck Institute for Evolutionary Anthropology
sources.notes: Classification reference for a skeleton-tier entry.
